name: get_agent_context
description: Get full session context in one call: user profile, active goals, today's nutrition totals and remaining macros, and the last 5 meals. Call this at the start of every session before doing anything else.

name: quick_log_meal
description: Log a meal from a plain-text description in one shot. Estimates macros using AI and immediately saves the meal. Use this as the default way to log food — no two-step flow needed. Always include source: 'openclaw'.

description.description: Plain-text description of what was eaten. Be specific about portions (e.g. '8oz grilled chicken breast, 1 cup white rice, steamed broccoli'). Max 1000 characters.

meal_type.description: Meal type. If omitted, the AI will infer it from the description.

logged_at.description: ISO 8601 datetime when the meal was eaten (e.g. '2026-05-01T12:30:00-05:00'). Defaults to now if omitted.

name: get_daily_nutrition
description: Get today's macro totals, goal progress, and remaining macros. Call this before logging to check how much is left, or after logging to report back to the user.

date.description: Date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Defaults to today if omitted.

name: get_weekly_nutrition
description: Get a weekly nutrition overview: today's totals, week averages, days remaining in the week, and goal adherence. Use this for weekly check-ins or when the user asks how they're tracking overall.

name: estimate_meal
description: Estimate macros for a food description WITHOUT logging it. Use when you want to preview macros before deciding whether to log, or when the user wants to know the macros of a food without logging it.

name: log_meal
description: Log a meal with exact macro values. Use this when you already have confirmed macros (e.g. after calling estimate_meal and the user approves). For plain-text logging, prefer quick_log_meal instead.

items.description: Array of food items with exact macros

name: list_meals
description: List logged meals with optional filters. Use to look up what was already logged today or on a specific date.

name: scan_meal
description: Log a meal from a photo. Send a base64-encoded image and get back macro-estimated items. Use when the user shares a photo of their food.
